content: * Polyphenols neutralize free radicals by donating electrons to superoxide, hydrogen peroxide, and peroxyl radicals.
* Flavanols, flavanones, flavonols, hydroxycinnamic acids, and anthocyanins are examples of polyphenolic compounds.
* In a randomized, controlled, crossover trial, 51 adults aged 60 and older experienced improved cognitive performance after consuming polyphenol-rich foods.
* A 2021 study found that consuming high-polyphenol olive oil for three weeks reduced plasma concentrations of oxidized LDL cholesterol and C-reactive protein compared to low-polyphenol olive oil.
* Cognitive performance begins to decline in middle-age years, and dietary modifications like increasing polyphenol-rich berry consumption may help improve or reverse these declines.
* Quercetin and fisetin have not been proven to reverse senescence, and green tea does not promote autophagy.
* Taking large doses of direct antioxidants like vitamins C and E close to exercise time can reduce physiological adaptations to exercise.
* Indirect antioxidants like resveratrol activate genetic pathways for the production of endogenous antioxidants, which may not significantly affect exercise adaptations.
